text: Captain said, “but you’re unknowns to us. Which means we have to err on the side of caution until we can determine if you’re a threat or not .” “I can’t leave her. I won’t.” Ronin was the one wasting time now. What choice did he have, here? She’d die if he did nothing . “My name’s Cooper. Captain Edward Cooper. You have my word that she will receive the best care we can offer. The quicker you cooperate, the quicker we can take care of her .” An electric crackle skittered across Ronin’s cheek. He nodded, once, and extended his arms. Walker and Garrison stepped forward and carefully collected Lara . It’s the only chance she has , he told himself, unable to shift his optics off her until she was carried out of his vision . “Captain Cooper,” Newton said, “I can assure you that I will vouch for — ” The Captain raised a fist, silencing Newton. “You’re unknowns .” A door — heavy steel, by the sound — opened somewhere behind the spotlight. More boots marched over the concrete floor, and soon six more armed soldiers stood in the corridor, surrounding Ronin and Newton . “These gentlemen will escort you inside. You will keep your hands to yourselves. They will not answer questions, so don’t bother asking. Any sudden movements that can be interpreted as hostile will be treated as such .” The soldiers took position — four behind and two ahead, all slightly to either side . “It may be of interest to you, especially if you’re considering doing any of these gentlemen harm, that an undisclosed number of them are bots. Just as physically capable as both of you, and they have rifles. I trust you’ll behave.” Cooper stepped aside, waving the escort forward . Ronin followed the soldiers in front of him automatically, his processors dominated by Lara. Where was she now? Would she be all right? He couldn’t begin to guess at the size of this facility, couldn’t be certain he’d find her on his own . They marched through the doorway, steel door slamming shut behind them. The corridor narrowed beneath a low, arched ceiling, but the lights were functioning and the floor was free of dirt. Bundles of pipe and conduit ran along the walls . Ronin couldn’t dismiss the possibility that he’d never see Lara again. That all the time he’d spent with her was all he’d ever have; those moments would be the only ones they’d ever share. Either one of them — or both — might die before seeing the other . There’d been no goodbye. No parting words of love. Only the pain on her face, the agony of her cries. Why was that last, terrible event so much more powerful than everything else? Why did it overshadow all the rest of their time together ? The corridor hit an intersection, and Ronin turned left with the group, storing step counts and estimated measurements in his memory. They were closer to the ruined base on the surface , now . “Where is she?” he asked . “You guys actually walk around out there?” one of the soldiers asked, as though he hadn’t heard Ronin’s question. “Like, through the storms and everything ?” “Doesn’t affect bots the same,” another answered. There was something about his posture, the way he walked, that wasn’t entirely human. The pointed stripes on his chest meant he was a sergeant . “That is correct,” Newton said, “but it does have a number of averse — ” “I just ain’t been off the base in a long time,” the first soldier interrupted . “You’ve never been of the base, Ramirez,” the Sergeant said, and the group fell silent . Their boots thumped over the floor, undercut by the clacking of Newton’s bare metal feet, and their equipment harnesses jingled softly. The lights hummed, barely audible beneath the other noise. None of it could distract Ronin from thoughts of Lara. His processors tumbled through hundreds of thousands of possibilities . “Where is she?” he asked again . “ Who ?” “The woman I brought here .” “Oh, the one that looked like pounded meat?” Ramirez asked. “You do that ?” Anger flared through Ronin’s systems, catching him off-guard. To hear Lara referred to in that way — so close to Warlord’s meatbag — for someone to suggest Ronin might have had something to do with, as though he would ever harm her … Newton gripped Ronin’s forearm, squeezing. Ronin dropped his gaze. They were here to save her. He had to keep that notion at the front of his mind . “Shut the fuck up, Ramirez,” three of the other soldiers said simultaneously . “Don’t talk about her that way again,” Ronin said, and pressed his lips together . “He didn’t mean anything by it,” the Sergeant assured. “Kid just doesn’t know how to think without speaking yet. Doc’s going to do all she can to fix your woman .” Before Ronin could respond, they came to another large, metal door. One of the leading soldiers stepped forward to open it. A wave of sound spilled out, dozens of voices and activities in a jumbled mess. He was ushered through, into a huge chamber filled with people going about a variety of tasks, many of them engaged in conversation as they worked . His optics swept over the space as he walked with the soldiers, who followed the set of colored lines painted on the floor. Most of the people were human, but many bots were interspersed in the crowd — not just synths, but older models similar to Greene. Everyone was well-groomed and clean, dressed in cared-for clothing. Ronin couldn’t even identify all the work they were doing, didn’t have names or functions associated with many of their tools. He’d never seen anything of the sort during his travels . Overhead, walkways hung from the high ceiling. Several more uniformed soldiers stood upon
